text:
While recent advances in neural forecasting have improved the performance of large-scale systems, long-horizon prediction remains a significant challenge. Two major hurdles in this area are the high volatility of forecasts and the prohibitive computational complexity of existing models. To address these issues, we introduce NHITS, a model that integrates two novel techniques: multi-rate data sampling and hierarchical interpolation.

These methods allow the model to assemble predictions sequentially, decomposing the input signal to emphasize components across a variety of frequencies and scales. Mathematically, we prove that hierarchical interpolation can efficiently approximate arbitrarily long forecasting horizons when the underlying signal is smooth. 

We validated our approach through extensive experiments on large-scale benchmark datasets. NHITS consistently outperforms current state-of-the-art methods, achieving an average accuracy improvement of nearly 20% over the latest Transformer-based architectures. Remarkably, it does so while reducing computational time by an order of magnitude—completing tasks up to 50 times faster than its competitors. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/Nixtla/neuralforecast.
